value:
v1.12.2 shipped 2026-04-16; v1.12.x integrity chain (#76-#80, #87) DONE.

v1.13.0 in development. Milestone has 10 open issues after 2026-04-23 cleanup session.

SHIPPED ON ORIGIN/MAIN (awaiting release tag), all 2026-04-23:
- #92 MCP tool description audit (530d284) — 19 descriptions rewritten; core change is codex_get no longer advertising "list all entries" to cut the 48% empty-key pattern.
- #93 audit log data quality gaps (0e8f813, 745c5a3) — 'remove' op distinct from 'write'; bootstrapRate/writeBackRate labels say "MCP sessions"; CLI codex_run now passes rawKey for aliasResolved capture.
- #90 hardcoded 'ccli' hint (a3aeb85) — replaced with getBinaryName() at all three locations found in the area.
- #89 shape-invalid import bypass (171b0e7) — refactored section extraction: raw pick → explicit shape validation → typed extraction. +3 tests.
- #86 softprops/action-gh-release v2 → v3 (2b8d1cf) — last remaining Node-20 action in release.yml.
- #91 cross-session handoff protocol (dcd2712) — formalized context.next_session as a banner in codex_context. No new tool; discoverability closed via LLM instructions.
- Cleanup (fd791bc): retired session.last experiment, removed vestigial .codexcli/ gitignores; established "all codex data must be checked in" principle (saved to user's auto-memory).

STILL OPEN IN v1.13.0 (10):
Narrow follow-up:
- #94 codex_copy aliasResolved semantic — from the #93 audit.

Small feats / cleanups:
- #88 askPassword non-interactive path (CI/scripting support for encrypted entries)
- #39 audit stored entries for interpolation syntax landmines (investigative)

Layer 2 tools (calibration data available from the real-usage dataset):
- #82 ccli lint --seed-quality heuristic
- #83 ccli topology — co-occurrence analysis on audit.jsonl

Larger multi-session items (candidates to trim out of v1.13.0):
- #84 unified entry health (lifecycle/coldness/staleness)
- #45 entry attribution (who/what last modified)
- #50 npx codexcli zero-install
- #47 ccli diff — compare local vs committed
- #6 Fish/PowerShell shell completion

DOGFOODING: docs/dogfooding-real-usage.md (committed 2026-04-22) as Part Two. v1.13.0 shipping validates the dataset-driven planning approach — worth a short addendum post about the six-issue closure session.

NEXT DECISION: 10 issues is sizeable. Consider trimming the milestone — move #45/#50/#6/#84 to v1.14+ so v1.13.0 ships with the coherent agent-first narrative (#91/#92/#93) + small cleanups + Layer 2 if scoped. Alternative: keep and close everything sequentially; risk of scope creep.
